title: Madronella hypoleuca
taxonomy: Madronella hypoleuca is a species of flowering plant in the heath family, Ericaceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is classified as a shrub and is often found growing in dry areas with well-drained soil. Madronella hypoleuca is a member of the tribe Phyllodoceae, which includes other similar species of flowering plants. Within the Ericaceae family, Madronella hypoleuca is also classified as part of the subfamily Epacridoideae.

preparations: Madronella hypoleuca is a shrub native to Australia, and its herbal preparations have been used in traditional medicine by the Indigenous Australians. The leaves of Madronella hypoleuca are used to make a tea that is said to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
